_source: Curated from a saved LinkedIn webarchive (2026-06). NOT live-fetched — this file is the contract (content tokens / slugs for copy). Edit here; the site renders from these slugs.
name: Robert DeLanghe
headline: AI agents write the production code. I write the contract it runs against.
intro: I'm Bobby. I chase the parts of code that feel like math, and build the systems that keep AI agents inside the job you gave them.
summary: I build the layer between provenance and enforcement for agent-authored code: capability seams that bound what an agent can touch, and a signed-owner provenance chain checked in CI. Open-sourced as prx, the same discipline I ship in production at Aura.

experience.what: Capability security for AI agents: software delivered by agents, governed like infrastructure. The same discipline I ship at Aura, generalised into open tooling.
bullets: Build a capability-security toolchain (prx, guest-room, claude-box) that bounds an AI agent to the access it holds: authority is the handle, not a rule it's checked against.
Tie privileged actions to a signed owner and an auditable in-toto/SLSA provenance chain, enforced in CI, not by convention.
Reach for Rust at the system layer and in Wasm: door-peercred, git-ast, hooksmith.

experience.what: On Commerce, the team behind Aura's website, commerce-routing, and business-operations systems. I ship the integration and automation backbone, and built the contract-and-validation layer behind inventory-purchasing.
bullets: Replaced Aura's spreadsheet-driven supply plan with one source of truth that compiles into every downstream artifact: schemas, docs, tests, agent context. CI blocks anything that drifts out of sync.
Built the auditing in: generated outputs are checked for provenance and against their contract. Sign-off binds to an owner role enforced in CI, not to a person.

experience.what: Owned the quality gates and developer experience for a 20-engineer team, spanning static-analysis CI, a design system, and frontend performance.
bullets: Shipped static-analysis gating into the PR pipeline to block defect classes before merge. Over the same period, critical bugs dropped 20% and high-priority issues 25% across the 20-engineer team.

experience.what: Designed the events + revenue data layer for the arts foundation.
bullets: Drove event status from attached documents (certificate of occupancy, design, invoice) across a highly relational model, so state was derived from evidence rather than a human-set flag that could drift out of sync.

experience.what: Automated just-in-time production for the fabrication studio.
bullets: Cut edition-numbering errors from 10/month to zero by making the invalid state unrepresentable in the production workflow.

experience.what: Engineered the publishing pipeline for the manufacturing publication.
bullets: Designed the schema that moved unstructured spreadsheet data into a relational database, making malformed records unrepresentable at the data layer rather than caught downstream.

experience.what: Type theory in Haskell, using the type system to make invalid states unrepresentable. The intellectual root of the capability work: push correctness into the boundary so violations can't compile.
bullets: Completed a selective 12-week batch (a self-directed programming retreat); researched Haskell type theory and how a well-constrained type narrows the space of possible implementations.

seeking.label: Open to new roles
seeking.focus: Senior / founding engineering on agent infrastructure: agent safety and code provenance
seeking.detail: Senior engineering on teams building agentic products. I bring direct experience shipping agent-authored systems and the guardrails that make them trustworthy in production. Brooklyn · NYC · remote.
